prompt: A Meridian Training Operations system with main features including class scheduling, live-and-recorded session access, offline checkout with promotions, operational search, and data ingestion for reporting. The Vue.js web interface serves five roles: Administrator (sets locations, instructors, policies), Instructor (manages sessions and attendance), Learner (books and joins sessions, watches replays), Finance Analyst (handles payments, refunds, reconciliation exports), and Data Ops (configures ingestion jobs and monitors data health). Users browse a course calendar in their chosen time zone with weekly/monthly views, recurring sessions (for example, every Tuesday and Thursday at 6:30 PM), capacity limits (such as 20 seats), and configurable buffer times (default 10 minutes between sessions). Booking follows a clear state flow from request to confirm, reschedule, or cancel; reschedules are blocked within 2 hours of start time, and learner-initiated cancellations within 24 hours can be flagged for a policy fee. When a session is live, learners enter a local “room” view; once recorded, replay tiles appear with access rules such as “only enrolled learners” and “available for 30 days after 03/27/2026.” Attendance and viewing stats are shown immediately after a session, including minutes attended, late-join counts, and replay completion rate. Checkout supports automatic best-offer calculation using stack controls (mutual exclusion by default), with examples like 10% off, $25 off orders over $200, or buy-one-get-one for selected workshops, and the UI displays traceable discount explanations on the receipt screen. Staff can run advanced search with combined filters (invoice number, learner phone, enrollment status, date range, site, instructor), save up to 20 searches per user, apply quick filters, export to CSV/Excel with a 50,000-row limit per export, and navigate fast pagination with clear “results updated” feedback. The backend uses FastAPI to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the Vue.js frontend, enforcing server-side validation and storing all records in PostgreSQL for local persistence. Authentication is fully offline with username and password only; passwords must be at least 12 characters with complexity rules, sessions expire after 30 minutes of inactivity, and accounts lock for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts. Sensitive fields such as phone numbers are masked in most views and encrypted at rest, and audit logs capture who changed schedules, promotions, refunds, and access controls with 90-day retention. Payments are implemented as an offline payment ledger and interface layer that can accept LAN-based “payment result callbacks” from an on-prem terminal or simulator; callbacks must pass signature verification, orders auto-close after 30 minutes unpaid, refund requests track statuses through pending to completed, and a daily reconciliation export is generated at 2:00 AM for Finance review. A built-in job scheduler orchestrates recurring tasks (attendance rollups, exports, ingestion, reconciliation) with queue-based retries using exponential backoff, and the admin console provides job throughput, latency, and success rate; when failures exceed 2% over the last hour or a job is late by 10 minutes, the system raises an in-app alert and records aggregated error logs for troubleshooting. Multi-source ingestion runs entirely on local infrastructure, supporting Kafka/Flume/Logstash streams, batch file imports, and incremental CDC pulls from MySQL/PostgreSQL sources; each source has connectivity checks, configurable collection frequency and concurrency caps, and deterministic reprocessing controls to prevent duplicate records.
